# Exploratory data analysis with R4DS
Now that we have covered basic R programming and basic concepts in statistics, we are ready to dive into Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA). EDA is used to gain an understanding of a dataset and is an essential for anyone working with data. In this part of the course we will be using the course literature, [R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/), and the `tidyverse` package. The book is written by one of the authors of the `tidyverse` package, Hadley Wickham, along with Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, and Garrett Grolemund, so we are certain you are in good hands.
